sourceMovie.title: American Sniper
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit American Sniper for the same reason it broke through in 2014: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a action rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Clint Eastwood keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The war and action blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Lone Survivor
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If American Sniper clicked for you, Lone Survivor (2013) is a strong next stop: both trade in war and action, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after American Sniper?
faq.answer: Try Lone Survivor, 13 Hours: The Secret Soldiers of Benghazi, Tom Clancy's Without Remorse—each shares DNA with American Sniper while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is American Sniper?
faq.answer: American Sniper (2014) is best described as War, Action. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is American Sniper worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.4/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this war lane.
